title: Cost of Debt: If a company has a debt-to-equity ratio of 0.5, what...
summary: Calculate the weighted average cost of capital or cost of equity using CAPM and capital structure data.
prompt: If a company has a debt-to-equity ratio of 0.5, what does this imply about its capital structure?

options.text: Measures ability to meet short-term obligations

solution: A debt-to-equity ratio of 0.5 indicates that for every dollar of equity, the company has 50 cents of debt, implying that it has more equity than debt.
